question: An older `application.yaml` configures a SaaS connection via the now-deprecated `camunda.client.cluster-id`. Which property is its documented replacement?

a.text: The deprecation table for `camunda.client` maps `camunda.client.cluster-id` (env `CAMUNDA_CLIENT_CLUSTERID`) to `camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id` (env `CAMUNDA_CLIENT_CLOUD_CLUSTERID`).

b.text: `camunda.client.zeebe.*` is itself the deprecated Zeebe-client namespace; it does not host the SaaS cluster id.

c.text: `tenant-id` selects a multi-tenancy tenant for requests; it does not identify a SaaS cluster.

d.text: Per-worker defaults under `worker.defaults` cover worker behaviour (autocomplete, fetch variables, timeouts) and do not include the cluster id.

explanation: SaaS-specific identifiers such as the cluster id and region now live under the `camunda.client.cloud.*` namespace. The deprecated table maps `camunda.client.cluster-id` directly to `camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id` so existing configurations migrate cleanly.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
